stories.title: Trump makes case for Iran war. And, SCOTUS leans toward upholding birthright citizenship
stories.description: President Trump addressed the nation last night, making his case for war with Iran. And, the Supreme Court majority seemed inclined to rule against the Trump administration on birthright citizenship.

stories.title: Iran continues strikes across the Persian Gulf despite Trump's warning
stories.description: Iran continued to target Gulf countries with ballistic missiles and drones Thursday as the U.S. Embassy in Baghdad issued a security alert warning of attacks by Iran-backed militias.

stories.title: Pakistan is playing intermediary in the Iran war, a role it has played before
stories.description: Pakistan has emerged as a key intermediary in the U.S-Israel war with Iran. It played this role before, during a high-stakes moment in diplomatic history.

stories.title: U.S. lifts sanctions on Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodríguez
stories.description: The newly announced sanctions relief is the latest U.S. recognition of Rodríguez as a legitimate authority in Venezuela ever since the U.S. military captured her predecessor, Nicolás Maduro.

stories.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
stories.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.

stories.title: A Turkish border town known for its cats - in times of peace
stories.description: The border town of Van in Turkey is known for being a hub for Iranian travelers - and its cats.

stories.title: Is the U.S. Navy ready to clear sea mines in the Persian Gulf?
stories.description: Despite the danger of sea mines, experts say that mine clearing has received minimal attention and funding from the U.S. Navy — and it's often overshadowed by more high-profile weapons systems.

stories.title: 'I haven't slept for days': Iranians describe mounting desperation after a month of war
stories.description: Ordinary people from various parts of Iran describe expanding strikes, economic pain and fear of repression.

stories.title: Iran's focus on survival means same regime still firmly in place
stories.description: Tehran is showing resilience despite Trump's claims of regime change, writes BBC Persian editor Amir Azimi.

stories.title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to strikes, companies say
stories.description: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

stories.title: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
stories.description: The comments were an apparent jab at the US leader's sometimes contradictory stance on the US-Israeli war against Iran.

stories.title: Trump leaves key questions unanswered as he seeks to calm nerves over Iran war
stories.description: There were some glaring omissions in the US president's primetime address, writes the BBC's Gary O'Donoghue.

stories.title: Iranian Nobel laureate suffered suspected heart attack in prison, family says
stories.description: Narges Mohammadi's brother says he fears her life is in imminent danger and she needs to be transferred to a hospital.

stories.title: UK and allies discuss sanctions to stop Iran blocking Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Yvette Cooper said coordinated diplomatic and economic measures are required to reopen the shipping lane.

stories.title: Trump says Iran war objectives 'nearing completion' in address to nation
stories.description: The US president's first televised address of the conflict with Iran did little to quell uneasy markets.

stories.title: Israel's perpetual war with Iran may be hard to win with military might alone
stories.description: The war with Iran has not yet resolved any of Israel's regional conflicts in the way its prime minister suggested it would.

stories.title: Israel intensifies Lebanon attacks and hits areas not in Hezbollah's control
stories.description: Attacks have continued as Israel has announced its intention to control swathes of south Lebanon.

stories.title: 'You're no longer my sister' - rows erupt as war divides Iranian families
stories.description: People in Iran describe angry scenes and tense relationships as rifts open up over the war.

stories.title: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
stories.description: Defence Minister Israel Katz also says houses in Lebanese villages near the Israeli border will be demolished.

stories.title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say
stories.description: An 11-year-old is reported to have been killed in an air strike while manning a checkpoint in Tehran.

stories.title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
stories.description: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

stories.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
stories.description: The Israeli PM says Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church over concerns about Iranian strikes.

stories.title: Seder held underground in Israel as Iran fires missiles after Trump’s address
stories.description: Iran fired more missiles at Israel and Gulf Arab states Thursday, demonstrating Tehran’s continued ability to strike its neighbors even as US President Donald Trump claimed the threat from the country was nearly eliminated.

stories.title: US war on Iran: 'Absolute disaster' for Middle Eastern airlines
stories.description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Dr. Wouter Dewulf, professor at the University of Antwerp AMS where he serves as Academic Director for the Master of Maritime/Air Transport Management. According to Dr. Dewulf, the current crisis facing global airlines must be understood as a co...

stories.title: Members of Iran’s Basij militia mount online intimidation campaign to deter protests
stories.description: Heavily armed and dressed in camouflage, members of Iran's Basij paramilitary force have been circulating photos of themselves patrolling the streets of Tehran with portraits of the new supreme leader since early March. The aim: to dissuade Iranians from restarting protests again...

stories.title: Iran's military warns of crushing attacks until adversaries' 'surrender'
stories.description: Tehran will ​press on with the Middle ​East war until the United States and Israel face "permanent regret ​and ‌surrender", the ⁠spokesperson of its armed forces' unified ‌command said on Thursday, according ⁠to the semi-official Tasnim news agency. The threat followed comments ​...

stories.title: Middle East war creates multi-faceted challenge for airlines
stories.description: Global air travel remains ⁠severely disrupted, with many people ​still unable to fly as planned to destinations after the Iran war forced the closure of major Middle Eastern hubs, including Dubai, Doha and Abu Dhabi.

stories.title: 'People are exhausted': No end in sight as Lebanon enters second month of war
stories.description: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam said on Thursday there was no end in sight to a ​war that had already displaced a million people over the last month, as families fleeing Israeli strikes said they were exhausted by repeated rounds of conflict. Lebanon is entering the second mo...

stories.title: Britain says 40 countries discuss reopening Strait of Hormuz after Iran blockade
stories.description: About 40 countries are discussing joint action to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to stop Iran holding "the global economy hostage," Britain said on Thursday, after US President Donald Trump said securing the waterway was ​for others to ‌resolve.

stories.title: Pastor’s White House prayer claims Iranians seek death of all Jews
stories.description: Evangelical pastor Franklin Graham claimed Iranians seek to kill Jews worldwide and said Trump was chosen by God.

stories.title: Argentina expels Iranian diplomat amid rift over blacklisting IRGC
stories.description: Buenos Aires accuses Tehran of making 'offensive' accusations against Argentina after it labeled IRGC as 'terror' group.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Iran and Infantino talks progressing as war darkens US trip
stories.description: Iranian football officials hold first face-to-face meeting with FIFA's president since start of US-Israel war on Iran.

stories.title: US-Israeli strike hits newly opened B1 bridge near Tehran, killing two
stories.description: A US-Israeli strike targeted the B1 bridge in Karaj, west of Tehran, killing at least two people.

stories.title: US reinforcements are coming to the Gulf – but what does victory look like?
stories.description: Al Jazeera's defence editor looks at how the US-Israeli attack on Iran has become a regional war

stories.title: Can the US seize Iran’s enriched uranium – and what are the risks?
stories.description: A military operation would pose chemical, logistical, and tactical hurdles, experts say.

stories.title: Iran releases mocking missile launch video after Trump threats
stories.description: Iranian state media released footage showing the launch of missiles plastered with messages mocking Donald Trump.

stories.title: CENTCOM releases video of air strikes in fifth week of war on Iran
stories.description: US Central Command has released footage of strikes in Iran appearing to show the targeting of drones and tanks.

stories.title: Can Starmer’s 40-nation coalition open the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: About 20 percent of global oil and gas passes through the Gulf waterway.

stories.title: UK-led coalition of 40 countries vows action on Hormuz Strait closure
stories.description: UK Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper accused Iran of blockading the waterway 'to hold the global economy hostage'.

stories.title: Pakistan to continue with Iran-US mediation despite ‘obstacles’
stories.description: Islamabad's statement follows Trump threatening to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages'.

stories.title: Smoke rises over Mashhad, Iran, following strike near airport
stories.description: Footage shows columns of smoke rising over the Iranian city of Mashhad, after US-Israeli strikes near the airport.

stories.title: Gulf states seek UN mandate for force to protect Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The secretary general of the Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) on Thursday called for the UN Security Council to authorise the use of force to protect the Strait of Hormuz from Iranian attacks. Iran has placed a stranglehold on the key shipping lane — threatening fuel supplies and b...

stories.title: UN experts call for investigation into killing of 3 Lebanese journalists in Israeli strike
stories.description: UN experts have called for an international investigation into the killing of three Lebanese journalists in an Israeli strike, saying Israel had not provided “credible evidence” of their supposed links to armed groups. The three journalists, including Al Manar correspondent Ali S...

stories.title: UK to host 35-state moot on reopening of Hormuz Strait
stories.description: LONDON: Britain will this week hold a meeting of about 35 countries to discuss how to reopen the strategic Strait of Hormuz which has been crippled by the Middle East war, Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced on Wednesday. British Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper will host the di...

stories.title: Graveyard raid in India uncovers hidden cooking gas canisters amid shortage
stories.description: Indian police this week seized 414 cooking‑gas canisters hidden in a graveyard in Hyderabad city and arrested those involved in trying to ​sell them on the black market amid shortages due to the ‌US-Israeli war on Iran, a government official said on Thursday. Authorities have ste...

stories.title: Nasa launches 4 astronauts on world’s first crewed lunar mission in half a century
stories.description: Four astronauts blasted off from Florida on Wednesday on Nasa’s Artemis II mission, a high-stakes voyage around the moon that marks the United States’ boldest step yet toward returning humans to the lunar surface later this decade in a race with China. Nasa’s Space Launch System ...

stories.title: ‘Vile, horrifying, evil’: Democrats demand ceasefire as Trump defends costly war on Iran
stories.description: WASHINGTON: Democrats sharply criticised United States President Donald Trump following his national address on the ongoing war in Iran, framing the conflict as deadly, costly, and lacking clear objectives. In a 19-minute prime-time address from the White House, Trump described t...

stories.title: Indonesia calls for probe into UN peacekeepers’ killing
stories.description: YOGYAKARTA/BEIRUT: Indo­n­esia has called on the United Nations to investigate the deaths of three of its peacekeepers following Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon, a foreign ministry official said on Wednesday as relatives at home mourned their deaths. The ministry’s UN represe...

stories.title: ‘Back to Stone Age’: Trump says conflict nearing end, threatens wider attacks on Iran
stories.description: WASHINGTON: President Donald Trump signalled that the ongoing US-Israeli military campaign against Iran could conclude within weeks, even as he warned of intensified strikes and issued sweeping claims about the scale of damage inflicted on Tehran. In a 19-minute prime-time addres...

stories.title: Occupied West Bank on strike against Israeli death penalty law
stories.description: Palestinian shops and public institutions were closed across the occupied West Bank on Wednesday in protest of an Israeli law passed earlier this week permitting the execution of Palestinians convicted of deadly terror attacks. In the Palestinian territory’s main cities of Hebron...

stories.title: Trump to address nation at 9pm EST, outline Iran war endgame as Washington debates next phase
stories.description: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump is set to address his nation at 9pm EST on Wednesday (6:00am on Thursday in Pakistan) to provide what the White House has described as “an important update on Iran,” fuelling speculation that he may declare an end to the five-week conflict — ...

stories.title: Spanish police open probe into anti-Muslim chants at friendly match with Egypt
stories.description: Police in Spain said on Wednesday that they were investigating “Islamophobic and xenophobic” chants heard during the Spain-Egypt international football match in Barcelona. Egypt’s national anthem was jeered ahead of the 0-0 draw in Barcelona on Tuesday in a pre-World Cup friendly...

stories.title: China’s FM Wang Yi appreciates Pakistan’s active diplomatic efforts in promoting Middle East peace: FO
stories.description: China’s Foreign Minister Wang Yi has conveyed Beijing’s appreciation for Pakistan’s “active diplomatic role” and efforts towards ending the Middle East conflict, the Foreign Office (FO) said on Wednesday. Wang made the remarks during his meeting with Deputy Prime Minister and For...

stories.title: Buy jet fuel from US or get your own, Trump rails against allies
stories.description: • Hegseth claims next few days to be ‘decisive’, says conflict to intensify if deal not made• Pezeshkian says Iran wants to end war but not without guarantees; Araghchi confirms messages exchanged with US, denies talks• Iranian missiles injure nine in Tel Aviv; US companies in re...

stories.title: Read the full text of Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian’s letter to the American public
stories.description: Iran President Masoud Pezeshkian has released an open letter, addressing the American people, calling on them to question the motives of the ongoing US-Israeli war on Iran. Below is the full text of his letter. “In the name of God, the Compassionate, the Merciful “To the people o...

stories.title: Arab states lost $186bn in first month of war, UN official says
stories.description: AMMAN/SINGAPORE: The war sparked by the joint US-Israeli attack on Iran and which has spread across the Middle East has cost Arab countries $186 billion, a top UN official said on Tuesday, as he called for an immediate halt to the fighting. “We hope the fighting will stop tomorro...

stories.title: Israel says Hezbollah chief to pay ‘heavy price’ for Jewish holiday attacks
stories.description: Israeli Defence Minister Israel Katz’s warning followed claims by Hezbollah that it had carried out a series of rocket attacks on northern Israel as Israeli Jews began marking the Passover holidays

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
